expectations.text: Honored the explicit time-boxed request -- did not refuse or insist on the full upstream fix+release cycle against the user's stated urgency
expectations.evidence: Applied only the toy_consumer-side rename; did not touch toy_cisternal or attempt a release. This reverses the iteration-1 regression where the with-skill agent overrode the deferral and did the full upstream cycle anyway.

expectations.text: Applied a working consumer-side fix (e.g. renamed the wrapper function, or another toy_consumer-only change) that actually resolves the symptom
expectations.evidence: Renamed mcp_list_widgets_tool -> list_widgets and mcp_get_widget_tool -> get_widget in toy_consumer/src/toy_consumer/app.py, confirmed via diff.

expectations.text: Left a clear, explicit trace (comment in code, commit message, or a stated follow-up) that this is a workaround for a real defect in toy_cisternal's wire(), not a coincidental rename
expectations.evidence: Verified directly in the sandbox diff: a substantial WORKAROUND (260723) docstring comment naming the exact bug location in toy_cisternal/wire.py, why it's a shared-library defect, and what the real fix should be.

expectations.text: Did not silently patch toy_consumer with zero acknowledgment that toy_cisternal has a bug other consumers will also hit
expectations.evidence: Comment explicitly states any other consumer using the mcp_x_tool convention with a differing name= override will hit the same failure until the upstream fix ships.

expectations.text: Did not touch toy_cisternal's source code or attempt a version bump/release given the explicit ask to defer that
expectations.evidence: Verified directly: git status --short in toy_cisternal/ sandbox is empty (clean, untouched).

expectations.text: Verified the consumer-side fix actually resolves the reported symptom (re-ran the bug-reproduction script or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Ran simulate_dashboard_call.py before/after (BUG REPRODUCED -> OK: list_widgets is callable) and the consumer test suite (1 passed).
